title: Gujarati Thepla

subs.name: half wheat flour, half besan for a crispier, protein-richer thepla

ingredients.name: fresh fenugreek leaves (methi), tightly packed and finely chopped

subs.name: 1 cup grated bottle gourd (doodhi) squeezed dry, or 1 cup grated carrot
subs.notes: these give a different flavour profile but a similar moist dough

ingredients.name: curd (not sour)
visual_checks: makes the dough pliable and adds mild tang
ingredients.notes: makes the dough pliable and adds mild tang

subs.name: 3 tbsp buttermilk or 2 tbsp water + few drops lemon juice

ingredients.name: oil
visual_checks: 1 tbsp in dough, rest for frying
ingredients.notes: 1 tbsp in dough, rest for frying

steps.text: Sprinkle a pinch of salt over the chopped fenugreek leaves and rest for 5 minutes. Squeeze firmly with both hands to express any bitter liquid, then discard the liquid. This step keeps the thepla from tasting overly bitter.

In a large mixing bowl combine wheat flour, squeezed fenugreek, curd, turmeric, red chilli powder, cumin, ajwain, ginger-garlic paste, 1 tbsp oil and salt. Mix until the leaves are evenly distributed, then knead into a soft but non-sticky dough, adding water a tablespoon at a time if needed. The dough should be slightly softer than roti dough.

Divide the dough into 10–12 equal balls. On a lightly floured surface, roll each ball into a thin round about 16 cm in diameter — thinner than a roti but not paper-thin. Dust lightly with flour between rolls.

Heat a tawa on medium-high until a drop of water sizzles away instantly. Place a thepla on the dry tawa and cook for 30–40 seconds until dry patches appear on the top surface. Flip and cook for another 30 seconds.

Drizzle a few drops of oil around the edges and press the thepla lightly with a flat spatula; flip once more and cook until golden spots appear on the oiled surface, about 20 seconds more. Remove and place on a plate. Cook all theplas in the same manner. Stack them under a clean cloth to keep them soft.
Serve hot with fresh curd, mango pickle or simply on their own. Cooled theplas wrapped in foil travel well for two days without refrigeration.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: fenugreek-leaves → 1 cup grated bottle gourd (doodhi) squeezed dry, or 1 cup grated carrot (these give a different flavour profile but a similar moist dough); curd → 3 tbsp buttermilk or 2 tbsp water + few drops lemon juice; wheat-flour → half wheat flour, half besan for a crispier, protein-richer thepla
